message:
Add Criterion microbenchmarks for the reliability pipeline (bd-vvmd.6.6)

Why: Performance budgets in AGENTS.md define hard limits (hook decision
<1ms, worker selection <10ms) but there was no automated way to track
these latencies over time. Without benchmarks, performance regressions
in the hot path could accumulate undetected until they breach SLO
thresholds in production.

What changed:
  - rch-common/benches/reliability_bench.rs: New Criterion benchmark
    suite covering:
      * Scenario spec construction and serialization
      * Error catalog lookups (all codes, single code, entry generation)
      * API error/response envelope creation and serialization
      * Reliability phase event creation and JSONL serialization
      * Process triage policy evaluation
      * Scenario report construction and serialization
      * Hook protocol output serialization
      * Full pipeline simulation (classification -> spec -> triage -> response)

  - rch-common/Cargo.toml: Register the new [[bench]] target with
    harness = false (required for Criterion benchmarks).

These benchmarks run as part of the nightly reliability suite
(--bench reliability_bench -- --quick) to detect regressions early.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -65,6 +65,10 @@ harness = false
 name = "ui_bench"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "reliability_bench"
+harness = false
+
 [lints.rust]
 # Use "deny" instead of "forbid" to allow tests to manipulate environment variables
 # (set_var/remove_var are unsafe in Rust 2024 edition)
